assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md: '**Classification:** code change request' and '## Action\nSub-task created to address this feedback'. subtask-1.md exists with content addressing comment 30001. report.md: '1 code change request (comment 30001: transaction wrapping); sub-task created.'

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: '**Classification:** suggestion'. Classification Reasoning states: 'the reviewer proposes an optimization (adding a partial index) that would improve query performance but is not strictly required for correctness. The language uses "should also add" which suggests a recommended improvement rather than identifying a bug or broken behavior.' This explicitly references the suggestive language patterns ('should also add') mentioned in the assertion.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md: '**Classification:** nit' and '## Action\nNo sub-task created. Minor style feedback that does not affect correctness.' No subtask file exists for comment 30003 (only subtask-1.md which addresses comment 30001).

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md: '**Classification:** question' and '## Action\nNo sub-task created. This asks for clarification; no code change is directly requested.' No subtask file exists for comment 30004.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 10), '## Implementation Notes' (line 13), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 19). All sections have substantive content.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md contains '## Review Context\n**Original review comment (ID 30001) by reviewer-a on `modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs` line 60:**' followed by the full quoted comment text: '> The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), one sub-task description (subtask-1.md), and a report (report.md). No code files (.rs, .sql, .toml, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report and review files are all analytical/descriptive documents, not code modifications.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md references all four: '1 code change request (comment 30001: transaction wrapping); sub-task created. 1 suggestion (30002: index), 1 nit (30003: context message), 1 question (30004: GET behavior)'. All comments were processed.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merge, or merge commands. The overall result is 'WARN' with issues requiring attention listed. The report is purely analytical with no merge actions.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file with 5 integration tests; no existing tests modified or removed |'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a 'Convention upgrade analysis' paragraph: 'The suggestion was evaluated for upgrade to a code change request under Check 1 (Convention Upgrade) of the Style/Conventions sub-agent. No CONVENTIONS.md content was available for this repository, and the PR diff contains only the new migration file — there are no other migration files visible in the diff to establish a codebase pattern of index creation alongside column additions. Without a documented convention or a demonstrated codebase pattern, the suggestion cannot be upgraded. It remains classified as a suggestion.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: '## Action\nNo sub-task created. The suggestion is not backed by a documented convention or demonstrated codebase pattern.' Only subtask-1.md exists in outputs (for comment 30001). report.md confirms: '1 suggestion (30002: index) ... no sub-tasks.'

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row: '| Test Quality | PASS | All tests are distinct (no repetitive patterns), all have doc comments. Eval Quality: N/A |'. The 'Eval Quality: N/A' is explicitly stated within the Test Quality check.
